palette_hint: pale slate background (#e8eaed), dark charcoal text (#1e1e24), rust-orange accent (#b5451b) for links and headings
type_style: system serif (Georgia) for headings and pull quotes, system sans-serif for body text, normal weight, comfortable 1.7 line-height
description: A personal blog by a mountaineer and essayist named Petra Voss, documenting climbs, slow travel, and ideas encountered along the way.
constraints: single centered column with max-width of 660px, generous top and bottom padding
navigation is a plain horizontal list of text links at the top, active page shown in rust-orange with no underline
thin 1px solid #c0c4cc horizontal rules separate every major section
no images, no cards, no boxes — typography only on the flat slate background
post titles are rust-orange serif, dates are small gray sans-serif above each title

page_specs.journal: The main feed page listing Petra's 6 most recent journal entries, each showing a small gray date, a rust-orange serif title, and a two-sentence excerpt followed by a plain 'Read more' text link. A short italicized site tagline — 'Writing from elevation and elsewhere' — sits just below the site name at the top.
page_specs.essays: A vertical list of 7 long-form essay titles with their publication date and a single-line subject tag (e.g., 'solitude', 'cartography', 'weather') in gray italic. No excerpts — just the title as a rust-orange link, date, and tag.
page_specs.field-notes: Brief dispatches from the trail: 8 entries listed chronologically, each with a date, a bold location name (e.g., 'Chamonix Valley, France'), and two to three lines of raw, unpolished observation text. No titles, no links — just the notes themselves separated by horizontal rules.
page_specs.reading-list: A curated list of 10 books that shaped Petra's thinking, organized under three plain-text headings: 'Mountains', 'Philosophy', and 'Fiction'. Each entry shows the book title in bold, author name in normal weight, and one sentence explaining why it matters to her.
page_specs.about: Three paragraphs introducing Petra Voss: her background as a geologist turned writer, the peaks she has summited, and what she hopes readers take from this blog. Ends with a plain-text email address and a note that she replies slowly but genuinely.
